channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Satellite images show Iran school strike hit more buildings than earlier reported
item.description: The images suggest that precision munitions struck other buildings, including a clinic that was also inside the complex.
item.content:encoded: The images suggest that precision munitions struck other buildings, including a clinic that was also inside the complex.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: A Biden-era rule sought to stabilize child care. Why Trump wants it gone
item.description: The Trump administration has proposed repealing a Biden-era rule that required states to change how they pay out child care subsidies, citing the potential for fraud.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration has proposed repealing a Biden-era rule that required states to change how they pay out child care subsidies, citing the potential for fraud.

(Image credit: Nadezhda1906)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: The Pentagon has now ID'd all 6 U.S. soldiers who died in a Kuwait attack
item.description: The last two names of the six U.S. soldiers killed in a Kuwait attack have been released by the Pentagon, and they are from California and Iowa. They died Sunday when a drone hit a command center in Port Shuaiba, Kuwait.
item.content:encoded:
The last two names of the six U.S. soldiers killed in a Kuwait attack have been released by the Pentagon, and they are from California and Iowa. They died Sunday when a drone hit a command center in Port Shuaiba, Kuwait.

(Image credit: Charlie Neibergall)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Pentagon releases names of 4 U.S. service members killed in Iran war
item.description: The Pentagon has identified four of the six U.S. service members killed in the first attacks on Iran.
item.content:encoded: The Pentagon has identified four of the six U.S. service members killed in the first attacks on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: White House begins arranging flights for Americans stranded in the Middle East
item.description: The Trump administration says it is now starting to arrange military and charter flights to help Americans who have been stranded in the Middle East since the war with Iran began.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration says it is now starting to arrange military and charter flights to help Americans who have been stranded in the Middle East since the war with Iran began.
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith

item.title: The U.S. sinks Iran's ships and slams its missile launchers as the war enters Day 5
item.description: Israel's military said it had begun a "broad wave of strikes" in Tehran on Wednesday morning. U.S. officials touted early gains, while Democrats warned the war could widen.
item.content:encoded:
Israel's military said it had begun a "broad wave of strikes" in Tehran on Wednesday morning. U.S. officials touted early gains, while Democrats warned the war could widen.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: On the brink of death, a woman is saved by a stranger and his family
item.description: In 1982, Jean Muenchrath and her boyfriend went mountaineering in California's Sierra Nevada. Then, an unexpected storm veered them dangerously off course. Luckily, their unsung hero found them.
item.content:encoded: In 1982, Jean Muenchrath and her boyfriend went mountaineering in California's Sierra Nevada. Then, an unexpected storm veered them dangerously off course. Luckily, their unsung hero found them.
item.dc:creator: Autumn Barnes

item.title: ICE has spun a massive surveillance web. We talked to people caught in it
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security, which oversees ICE and Border Patrol, is using a broad web of surveillance tools — purchased as its budget has ballooned under this administration — to monitor, apprehend and intimidate the people it seeks to deport and the U.S. citizens critical of its policies.
item.content:encoded: The Department of Homeland Security, which oversees ICE and Border Patrol, is using a broad web of surveillance tools — purchased as its budget has ballooned under this administration — to monitor, apprehend and intimidate the people it seeks to deport and the U.S. citizens critical of its policies.
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf

item.title: As Paralympics approach, U.S. skier Sydney Peterson balances training and research
item.description: Sydney Peterson is among the U.S. athletes heading to the 2026 Winter Paralympics. A neuroscientist in training, Peterson is studying movement disorders, similar to her own condition.
item.content:encoded:
Sydney Peterson is among the U.S. athletes heading to the 2026 Winter Paralympics. A neuroscientist in training, Peterson is studying movement disorders, similar to her own condition.

(Image credit: Emily Chen-Newton)
item.dc:creator: Emily Chen-Newton

item.title: Family sues after researcher's death, alleging university's probe led to suicide
item.description: The suicide of a Chinese American cancer researcher has raised painful questions over alleged discrimination against Asian scientists in the U.S.
item.content:encoded: The suicide of a Chinese American cancer researcher has raised painful questions over alleged discrimination against Asian scientists in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng
